response.justification: Bogdan is a broadly experienced, craftsmanship-oriented engineer, but his profile diverges sharply from this role's core requirements. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, far short of the required 8 years, and his large-scale systems exposure is about two years of low-latency DLang microservices at Sociomantic rather than 5 years building cloud infrastructure or compute platforms. His recent depth is in TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, and he has no formal technical-leadership role leading cloud or DevOps engineers, so he does not meet the minimum qualifications for a C++-centric cloud compute platform lead.
strengths: Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering-practice foundation: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and testing tooling authorship
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing through workshops, courses, and open-source project leadership
concerns: Professional C++ experience is under one year versus the required 8 years
Career depth is in web/full-stack TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, not cloud compute platforms or hardware/compute infrastructure
No formal technical-leadership role managing priorities, deliverables, or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers

key_factors.factor: Limited large-scale infrastructure and cloud platform experience
